title: Federal Insurance Office
summary: The Federal Insurance Office (FIO) is a Treasury bureau established under Title V of the Dodd-Frank Act, with authority to monitor the insurance sector and represent the U.S. on international insurance matters. This is a static institutional description page with no current market-relevant content, data releases, or policy announcements.
key_points: FIO was established under Title V of the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act.
FIO has authority to monitor all aspects of the insurance sector.
FIO monitors access to affordable non-health insurance products for underserved communities.
FIO represents the United States on prudential aspects of international insurance matters at the International Association of Insurance Supervisors.
FIO serves as an advisory member of the Financial Stability Oversight Council.
FIO assists the Treasury Secretary with administration of the Terrorism Risk Insurance Program.
why_it_matters: This source is not relevant to macro catalysts or market structure analysis. It is a general informational page describing FIO's statutory authorities and does not contain any current data, rate decisions, economic releases, or market-moving information.
